items.title: Singly-linked list
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  H["head"] --> A["val: A | next →"]
  A --> B["val: B | next →"]
  B --> C["val: C | next →"]
  C --> N["None"]
items.caption: Each node holds a value and a pointer to the next. Walking to index i is O(i).

items.title: Doubly-linked list
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  H["head"] --> A["← prev | A | next →"]
  A <--> B["← prev | B | next →"]
  B <--> C["← prev | C | next →"]
  C --> N["None"]
  T["tail"] --> C
items.caption: Backward pointers let you delete any node in O(1) if you already hold a reference — the trick used by LRU caches.

items.title: Insert in the middle (with a node reference)
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  subgraph Before
    A1["A"] --> B1["B"] --> C1["C"]
  end
  subgraph After["Insert X between A and B"]
    A2["A"] --> X["X"] --> B2["B"] --> C2["C"]
  end
  Before --> After
items.caption: Only two pointers change — O(1) — versus an O(n) shift in an array.
